content: read reviews/798.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/798.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/798.md.

1тЖТ# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist
     2тЖТ
     3тЖТEvery item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4тЖТwithout an explicit justification.
     5тЖТ
     6тЖТSources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ┬з1тАУ7),
     7тЖТ[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
     8тЖТ`FormalConjectures/Util/`.
     9тЖТ
    10тЖТ---
    11тЖТ
    12тЖТ## 1. Copyright Header
    13тЖТ
    14тЖТ- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
    15тЖТ  Lines 1тАУ15 contain the exact Apache 2.0 copyright block with no leading blank line.
    16тЖТ- [x] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).
    17тЖТ  Year reads `2026`.

    19тЖТ## 2. Import Statement
    20тЖТ

    23тЖТ- [x] No other imports are present.
    24тЖТ
    25тЖТ## 3. Module Docstring
    26тЖТ
    27тЖТ- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    28тЖТ  Lines 19тАУ33.
    29тЖТ- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Сs Problem 798`.
    30тЖТ- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
    31тЖТ  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/798](https://www.erdosproblems.com/798)`
    32тЖТ- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    33тЖТ  Describes $t(n)$ as the minimum number of points in $\{1,\ldots,n\}^2$ whose pairwise lines cover all grid points, states the estimation question, notes the lower bound by Erd┼СsтАУPurdy and the resolution by Alon.
    34тЖТ- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
    35тЖТ  `[Al91]` Alon (1991) is cited.
    36тЖТ
    37тЖТ## 4. Namespace
    38тЖТ
    39тЖТ- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos798 ... end Erdos798`).
    40тЖТ  Lines 35 and 70.
    41тЖТ- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos798`).
    42тЖТ- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces.
    43тЖТ  N/A тАФ no `open` statements needed; the file works directly with `тДд`, `тДХ`, `тДЭ`, `Finset`, and `sInf` without opening namespaces.
    44тЖТ
    45тЖТ## 5. Custom Definitions
    46тЖТ
    47тЖТ- [x] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    48тЖТ  `IntGridCollinear` (lines 37тАУ38), `CoversIntGrid` (lines 42тАУ43), and `gridCoveringNumber` (lines 49тАУ50) all have docstrings.
    49тЖТ- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
    50тЖТ  `gridCoveringNumber` (line 51) is correctly marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf` over a set of naturals). `IntGridCollinear` and `CoversIntGrid` return `Prop` and are computable тАФ no annotation needed.
    51тЖТ- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
    52тЖТ- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
    53тЖТ  `IntGridCollinear` and `CoversIntGrid` are Props in `UpperCamelCase`.
    54тЖТ- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (e.g., `normalizedPrimeGap`).
    55тЖТ  `gridCoveringNumber` returns `тДХ` and uses `lowerCamelCase`.
    56тЖТ- [x] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
    57тЖТ  N/A тАФ no custom notation used.
    58тЖТ- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.
    59тЖТ  **NOTE (non-blocking).** Three new definitions (`IntGridCollinear`, `CoversIntGrid`, `gridCoveringNumber`) lack sanity-check tests. Consider adding `@[category test]` examples, e.g. verifying that three known-collinear points satisfy `IntGridCollinear`, or that `CoversIntGrid 1 S` holds for a trivial single-point set.
    60тЖТ
    61тЖТ## 6. Theorem Statements
    62тЖТ
    63тЖТ- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` or `lemma` keyword (not `def` or `example`).
    64тЖТ  Line 64: `theorem erdos_798`.
    65тЖТ- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_N` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    66тЖТ  `erdos_798` matches `Erdos798`.

    68тЖТ- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
    69тЖТ  Line 63: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
    70тЖТ- [x] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    71тЖТ  Lines 54тАУ62 provide a detailed docstring.
    72тЖТ- [x] Theorem docstrings should ideally reference the source paper(s).
    73тЖТ  Docstring references `[Al91]`.
    74тЖТ
    75тЖТ### `answer()` usage
    76тЖТ
    77тЖТThe original problem on erdosproblems.com reads: *"Estimate $t(n)$. In particular, is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?"*
    78тЖТ
    79тЖТThe formalization addresses the sub-question *"Is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?"* by asserting тИА ╬╡ > 0, тИГ NтВА, тИА n тЙе NтВА, t(n) тЙд ╬╡┬╖n. The phrase "Is it true that..." is a yes/no question (Case 1). Since Alon resolved this affirmatively, the checklist requires `answer(True) тЖФ P`.
    80тЖТ
    81тЖТ- [ ] **ISSUE: Case 1 applies but `answer()` is not used.** The formalized statement directly corresponds to the yes/no sub-question "Is it true that t(n) = o(n)?". Per Case 1 of the checklist, since the answer is yes, it should use:
    82тЖТ  ```lean
    83тЖТ  theorem erdos_798 :
    84тЖТ      answer(True) тЖФ
    85тЖТ      тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
    86тЖТ      тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
    87тЖТ        (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
    88тЖТ    sorry
    89тЖТ  ```
    90тЖТ  **Current** (Case 3 format тАФ bare assertion):
    91тЖТ  ```lean
    92тЖТ  theorem erdos_798 :
    93тЖТ      тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
    94тЖТ      тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
    95тЖТ        (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
    96тЖТ    sorry
    97тЖТ  ```
    98тЖТ
    99тЖТ**Common rules for all cases with `answer()`:**
   100тЖТ
   101тЖТ- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
   102тЖТ  N/A currently (no `answer()` used), but the quantifiers are already in the statement body, so adding `answer(True) тЖФ` in front would satisfy this rule.
   103тЖТ
   104тЖТ## 7. Category Attribute
   105тЖТ
   106тЖТ- [x] Every `theorem`, `lemma`, and `example` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
   107тЖТ  `@[category research solved]` on the sole theorem.
   108тЖТ- [x] The value is one of the valid categories.
   109тЖТ  `research solved` is valid тАФ Alon [Al91] proved $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$ in 1991, confirming $t(n) = o(n)$, but the proof is not formally verified.
   110тЖТ- [x] A `research open` theorem must contain `sorry` in its proof body.
   111тЖТ  N/A тАФ category is `research solved`.
   112тЖТ
   113тЖТ## 8. AMS Attribute
   114тЖТ
   115тЖТ- [x] Every `theorem`, `lemma`, and `example` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
   116тЖТ  `@[AMS 5 52]` on the sole theorem.
   117тЖТ- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
   118тЖТ  Two numbers: `5` (Combinatorics) and `52` (Convex and discrete geometry).
   119тЖТ- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**.
   120тЖТ  5 < 52.
   121тЖТ- [x] No duplicate numbers.
   122тЖТ- [x] Every number is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
   123тЖТ  Both `5` and `52` are in the valid set.
   124тЖТ
   125тЖТ## 9. Proof Bodies
   126тЖТ
   127тЖТ- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry` (not bare `sorry` or `by { sorry }`).
   128тЖТ  Lines 67тАУ68: `... := by\n  sorry` тАФ acceptable multi-line form.
   129тЖТ- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
   130тЖТ- [x] No `sorry` in any file under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
   131тЖТ  N/A тАФ this file is under `deepmind/`.
   132тЖТ- [x] Do **not** include informal proof sketches for open research problems.
   133тЖТ  Category is `research solved`; no informal proof sketch is included.
   134тЖТ- [x] Avoid `native_decide`.
   135тЖТ  Not used.
   136тЖТ
   137тЖТ## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
   138тЖТ
   139тЖТ- [ ] **NOTE (non-blocking).** The original problem has two parts: (1) *"Estimate t(n)"* and (2) *"Is it true that t(n) = o(n)?"* Only the sub-question (2) is formalized. Erd┼СsтАУPurdy proved $t(n) \gg n^{2/3}$ and Alon proved $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$. Consider adding a variant `erdos_798.variants.tight_bounds` formalizing these sharper bounds, e.g.:
   140тЖТ  ```lean
   141тЖТ  theorem erdos_798.variants.tight_bounds :
   142тЖТ      (тИГ c > 0, тИАс╢а n in Filter.atTop,
   143тЖТ        c * (n : тДЭ)^((2 : тДЭ)/3) тЙд (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ)) тИз
   144тЖТ      (тИГ C > 0, тИАс╢а n in Filter.atTop,
   145тЖТ        (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд C * (n : тДЭ)^((2 : тДЭ)/3) * Real.log n) := by
   146тЖТ    sorry
   147тЖТ  ```
   148тЖТ- [x] All variants live in the **same file** as the main theorem.
   149тЖТ  N/A тАФ no variants present.
   150тЖТ- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
   151тЖТ  N/A.
   152тЖТ- [x] Variants that strengthen/weaken the main conjecture are documented with a brief docstring.
   153тЖТ  N/A.
   154тЖТ
   155тЖТ## 11. Code Quality
   156тЖТ
   157тЖТ- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
   158тЖТ- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`тИА`, `тИГ`, `тИИ`, `тДд`, `тДХ`, `тДЭ`, `тЙд`, `тЙе`, `тЖС`, `╬╡`, `тЙа`, `тИз`).
   159тЖТ- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   160тЖТ- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
   161тЖТ- [x] Lines are reasonably short (aim for <100 characters; break long lines).
   162тЖТ  All lines are under 100 characters.
   163тЖТ- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   164тЖТ
   165тЖТ## 12. Build Verification
   166тЖТ
   167тЖТ- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors**.
   168тЖТ  **Not verified** тАФ build not run during this review.

   170тЖТ  **Not verified** тАФ build not run during this review.
   171тЖТ
   172тЖТ---
   173тЖТ
   174тЖТ## Summary
   175тЖТ
   176тЖТ**Result: FAIL** (1 blocking issue)
   177тЖТ
   178тЖТ**Blocking issue (1):**
   179тЖТ
   180тЖТ1. **┬з6 `answer()` usage тАФ missing `answer(True) тЖФ` wrapper.** The original problem asks *"Is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?"* тАФ a yes/no question (Case 1). Since Alon resolved this affirmatively, the theorem should use `answer(True) тЖФ P` rather than a bare assertion `P`. Change to:
   181тЖТ   ```lean
   182тЖТ   theorem erdos_798 :
   183тЖТ       answer(True) тЖФ
   184тЖТ       тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
   185тЖТ       тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
   186тЖТ         (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
   187тЖТ     sorry
   188тЖТ   ```
   189тЖТ
   190тЖТ**Non-blocking suggestions (2):**
   191тЖТ
   192тЖТ1. **┬з5 тАФ Sanity-check tests for custom definitions.** Three new definitions (`IntGridCollinear`, `CoversIntGrid`, `gridCoveringNumber`) lack `@[category test]` sanity checks. Consider adding small test examples.
   193тЖТ2. **┬з10 тАФ Missing variant for tight estimation bounds.** The original problem's primary ask is *"Estimate t(n)"*. The known bounds $t(n) \gg n^{2/3}$ (Erd┼СsтАУPurdy) and $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$ (Alon) are not formalized. Consider adding a variant `erdos_798.variants.tight_bounds`.
   194тЖТ
   195тЖТ**Build verification** was not performed.
   196тЖТ
   197тЖТ**Otherwise:** The file is well-structured with correct copyright header, import, namespace, well-documented custom definitions with appropriate `noncomputable` marking, proper category/AMS attributes, detailed module docstring with bibliography, and clean code style. The mathematical formalization of $t(n) = o(n)$ via the standard ╬╡-NтВА characterization is correct and clean. The collinearity and grid-covering definitions are mathematically sound.
   198тЖТ

# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ┬з1тАУ7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
  Lines 1тАУ15 contain the exact Apache 2.0 copyright block with no leading blank line.
- [x] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).
  Year reads `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`, separated from the copyright block by exactly one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
  Lines 19тАУ33.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Сs Problem 798`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/798](https://www.erdosproblems.com/798)`
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
  Describes $t(n)$ as the minimum number of points in $\{1,\ldots,n\}^2$ whose pairwise lines cover all grid points, states the estimation question, notes the lower bound by Erd┼СsтАУPurdy and the resolution by Alon.
- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
  `[Al91]` Alon (1991) is cited.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos798 ... end Erdos798`).
  Lines 35 and 70.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos798`).
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces.
  N/A тАФ no `open` statements needed; the file works directly with `тДд`, `тДХ`, `тДЭ`, `Finset`, and `sInf` without opening namespaces.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
  `IntGridCollinear` (lines 37тАУ38), `CoversIntGrid` (lines 42тАУ43), and `gridCoveringNumber` (lines 49тАУ50) all have docstrings.
- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
  `gridCoveringNumber` (line 51) is correctly marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf` over a set of naturals). `IntGridCollinear` and `CoversIntGrid` return `Prop` and are computable тАФ no annotation needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
  `IntGridCollinear` and `CoversIntGrid` are Props in `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (e.g., `normalizedPrimeGap`).
  `gridCoveringNumber` returns `тДХ` and uses `lowerCamelCase`.
- [x] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
  N/A тАФ no custom notation used.
- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.
  **NOTE (non-blocking).** Three new definitions (`IntGridCollinear`, `CoversIntGrid`, `gridCoveringNumber`) lack sanity-check tests. Consider adding `@[category test]` examples, e.g. verifying that three known-collinear points satisfy `IntGridCollinear`, or that `CoversIntGrid 1 S` holds for a trivial single-point set.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` or `lemma` keyword (not `def` or `example`).
  Line 64: `theorem erdos_798`.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_N` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
  `erdos_798` matches `Erdos798`.
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
  Line 63: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
  Lines 54тАУ62 provide a detailed docstring.
- [x] Theorem docstrings should ideally reference the source paper(s).
  Docstring references `[Al91]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem on erdosproblems.com reads: *"Estimate $t(n)$. In particular, is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?"*

The formalization addresses the sub-question *"Is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?"* by asserting тИА ╬╡ > 0, тИГ NтВА, тИА n тЙе NтВА, t(n) тЙд ╬╡┬╖n. The phrase "Is it true that..." is a yes/no question (Case 1). Since Alon resolved this affirmatively, the checklist requires `answer(True) тЖФ P`.

- [ ] **ISSUE: Case 1 applies but `answer()` is not used.** The formalized statement directly corresponds to the yes/no sub-question "Is it true that t(n) = o(n)?". Per Case 1 of the checklist, since the answer is yes, it should use:
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_798 :
      answer(True) тЖФ
      тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
      тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
        (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
    sorry
  ```
  **Current** (Case 3 format тАФ bare assertion):
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_798 :
      тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
      тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
        (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
    sorry
  ```

**Common rules for all cases with `answer()`:**

- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
  N/A currently (no `answer()` used), but the quantifiers are already in the statement body, so adding `answer(True) тЖФ` in front would satisfy this rule.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem`, `lemma`, and `example` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
  `@[category research solved]` on the sole theorem.
- [x] The value is one of the valid categories.
  `research solved` is valid тАФ Alon [Al91] proved $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$ in 1991, confirming $t(n) = o(n)$, but the proof is not formally verified.
- [x] A `research open` theorem must contain `sorry` in its proof body.
  N/A тАФ category is `research solved`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem`, `lemma`, and `example` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
  `@[AMS 5 52]` on the sole theorem.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
  Two numbers: `5` (Combinatorics) and `52` (Convex and discrete geometry).
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**.
  5 < 52.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Every number is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
  Both `5` and `52` are in the valid set.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry` (not bare `sorry` or `by { sorry }`).
  Lines 67тАУ68: `... := by\n  sorry` тАФ acceptable multi-line form.
- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No `sorry` in any file under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
  N/A тАФ this file is under `deepmind/`.
- [x] Do **not** include informal proof sketches for open research problems.
  Category is `research solved`; no informal proof sketch is included.
- [x] Avoid `native_decide`.
  Not used.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [ ] **NOTE (non-blocking).** The original problem has two parts: (1) *"Estimate t(n)"* and (2) *"Is it true that t(n) = o(n)?"* Only the sub-question (2) is formalized. Erd┼СsтАУPurdy proved $t(n) \gg n^{2/3}$ and Alon proved $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$. Consider adding a variant `erdos_798.variants.tight_bounds` formalizing these sharper bounds, e.g.:
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_798.variants.tight_bounds :
      (тИГ c > 0, тИАс╢а n in Filter.atTop,
        c * (n : тДЭ)^((2 : тДЭ)/3) тЙд (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ)) тИз
      (тИГ C > 0, тИАс╢а n in Filter.atTop,
        (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд C * (n : тДЭ)^((2 : тДЭ)/3) * Real.log n) := by
    sorry
  ```
- [x] All variants live in the **same file** as the main theorem.
  N/A тАФ no variants present.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
  N/A.
- [x] Variants that strengthen/weaken the main conjecture are documented with a brief docstring.
  N/A.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`тИА`, `тИГ`, `тИИ`, `тДд`, `тДХ`, `тДЭ`, `тЙд`, `тЙе`, `тЖС`, `╬╡`, `тЙа`, `тИз`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (aim for <100 characters; break long lines).
  All lines are under 100 characters.
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors**.
  **Not verified** тАФ build not run during this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass.
  **Not verified** тАФ build not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: FAIL** (1 blocking issue)

**Blocking issue (1):**

1. **┬з6 `answer()` usage тАФ missing `answer(True) тЖФ` wrapper.** The original problem asks *"Is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?"* тАФ a yes/no question (Case 1). Since Alon resolved this affirmatively, the theorem should use `answer(True) тЖФ P` rather than a bare assertion `P`. Change to:
   ```lean
   theorem erdos_798 :
       answer(True) тЖФ
       тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
       тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
         (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
     sorry
   ```

**Non-blocking suggestions (2):**

1. **┬з5 тАФ Sanity-check tests for custom definitions.** Three new definitions (`IntGridCollinear`, `CoversIntGrid`, `gridCoveringNumber`) lack `@[category test]` sanity checks. Consider adding small test examples.
2. **┬з10 тАФ Missing variant for tight estimation bounds.** The original problem's primary ask is *"Estimate t(n)"*. The known bounds $t(n) \gg n^{2/3}$ (Erd┼СsтАУPurdy) and $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$ (Alon) are not formalized. Consider adding a variant `erdos_798.variants.tight_bounds`.

**Build verification** was not performed.

**Otherwise:** The file is well-structured with correct copyright header, import, namespace, well-documented custom definitions with appropriate `noncomputable` marking, proper category/AMS attributes, detailed module docstring with bibliography, and clean code style. The mathematical formalization of $t(n) = o(n)$ via the standard ╬╡-NтВА characterization is correct and clean. The collinearity and grid-covering definitions are mathematically sound.

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Сs Problem 798

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/798](https://www.erdosproblems.com/798)

Let $t(n)$ be the minimum number of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ such that the $\binom{t}{2}$
lines determined by these points cover all points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$.

Estimate $t(n)$. In particular, is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?

A problem of Erd┼Сs and Purdy, who proved $t(n) \gg n^{2/3}$.
Resolved by Alon [Al91] who proved $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$, confirming $t(n) = o(n)$.

[Al91] Alon, N., _A note on the covering number of $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$_ (1991).
-/

namespace Erdos798

/-- Three points in $\mathbb{Z} \times \mathbb{Z}$ are collinear if the cross product of the
displacement vectors from the first point is zero. -/
def IntGridCollinear (p q r : тДд ├Ч тДд) : Prop :=
  (q.1 - p.1) * (r.2 - p.2) = (r.1 - p.1) * (q.2 - p.2)

/-- A finite set $S$ of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ covers the grid if every point
in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ lies on a line determined by two distinct points of $S$. -/
def CoversIntGrid (n : тДХ) (S : Finset (тДд ├Ч тДд)) : Prop :=
  (тИА p тИИ S, 1 тЙд p.1 тИз p.1 тЙд тЖСn тИз 1 тЙд p.2 тИз p.2 тЙд тЖСn) тИз
  тИА (p : тДд ├Ч тДд), 1 тЙд p.1 тЖТ p.1 тЙд тЖСn тЖТ 1 тЙд p.2 тЖТ p.2 тЙд тЖСn тЖТ
    тИГ a тИИ S, тИГ b тИИ S, a тЙа b тИз IntGridCollinear a b p

/-- $t(n)$: the minimum number of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ whose pairwise lines
cover all $n^2$ grid points. -/
noncomputable def gridCoveringNumber (n : тДХ) : тДХ :=
  sInf {k : тДХ | тИГ S : Finset (тДд ├Ч тДд), S.card = k тИз CoversIntGrid n S}

/--
Erd┼Сs Problem 798 (proved by Alon [Al91]):

$t(n) = o(n)$, i.e., the minimum number of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ whose lines
cover all grid points is sublinear in $n$.

Equivalently: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
$t(n) \leq \varepsilon \cdot n$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
theorem erdos_798 :
    тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
    тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
      (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
  sorry

end Erdos798

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Сs Problem 798

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/798](https://www.erdosproblems.com/798)

Let $t(n)$ be the minimum number of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ such that the $\binom{t}{2}$
lines determined by these points cover all points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$.

Estimate $t(n)$. In particular, is it true that $t(n) = o(n)$?

A problem of Erd┼Сs and Purdy, who proved $t(n) \gg n^{2/3}$.
Resolved by Alon [Al91] who proved $t(n) \ll n^{2/3} \log n$, confirming $t(n) = o(n)$.

[Al91] Alon, N., _A note on the covering number of $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$_ (1991).
-/

namespace Erdos798

/-- Three points in $\mathbb{Z} \times \mathbb{Z}$ are collinear if the cross product of the
displacement vectors from the first point is zero. -/
def IntGridCollinear (p q r : тДд ├Ч тДд) : Prop :=
  (q.1 - p.1) * (r.2 - p.2) = (r.1 - p.1) * (q.2 - p.2)

/-- A finite set $S$ of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ covers the grid if every point
in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ lies on a line determined by two distinct points of $S$. -/
def CoversIntGrid (n : тДХ) (S : Finset (тДд ├Ч тДд)) : Prop :=
  (тИА p тИИ S, 1 тЙд p.1 тИз p.1 тЙд тЖСn тИз 1 тЙд p.2 тИз p.2 тЙд тЖСn) тИз
  тИА (p : тДд ├Ч тДд), 1 тЙд p.1 тЖТ p.1 тЙд тЖСn тЖТ 1 тЙд p.2 тЖТ p.2 тЙд тЖСn тЖТ
    тИГ a тИИ S, тИГ b тИИ S, a тЙа b тИз IntGridCollinear a b p

/-- $t(n)$: the minimum number of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ whose pairwise lines
cover all $n^2$ grid points. -/
noncomputable def gridCoveringNumber (n : тДХ) : тДХ :=
  sInf {k : тДХ | тИГ S : Finset (тДд ├Ч тДд), S.card = k тИз CoversIntGrid n S}

/--
Erd┼Сs Problem 798 (proved by Alon [Al91]):

$t(n) = o(n)$, i.e., the minimum number of points in $\{1, \ldots, n\}^2$ whose lines
cover all grid points is sublinear in $n$.

Equivalently: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
$t(n) \leq \varepsilon \cdot n$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
theorem erdos_798 :
    тИА ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тЖТ
    тИГ NтВА : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, n тЙе NтВА тЖТ
      (gridCoveringNumber n : тДЭ) тЙд ╬╡ * (n : тДЭ) := by
  sorry

end Erdos798
